title: We are ACCEPTING ESC APPLICANTS for Incoming Grade 7
content:
ESC (Educational Service Contracting) Certified
is a Department of Education (DepEd) partnership with private schools to provide tuition subsidies for Filipino students, aiming to decongest public junior high schools. Administered by the Private Education Assistance Committee (PEAC), it offers subsidy grants to Grade 7 students, covering fees of 11,000pesos through JHS journey.

Eligibility:

-Filipino citizen
-School age (12-18 years old) at the time of Grade 7 enrolment
-Incoming Grade 7 student
Coverage:
Four years of Junior High School starting in Grade 7

title: Youngest Competitor, Biggest Win Grade 1 BCT pupil dominates Gymnastics Competition
content:
Allison Grace Zeluff, a Grade 1 student of Brokenshire College Toril (BCT), proved that age is no barrier to excellence after an exceptional showing at the 4th Learn and Train Sports Academy (LTSA) Invitational held at the Ninoy Aquino Stadium in Malate, Manila.

title: Rally to Podium
content:
Kieffer Seth Ven and Lantis Jhae Soriano battled through intense rallies to seize the BRONZE medal in the Secondary Table Tennis Boys Doubles event at the DAVRAA Meet 2026.
The pair trained under in-house and official coach Princess Sarah Paragsa and Zoren Patrick Mendiola.
Congratulations and we are proud of you, Brokenshirians

title: Mastersful Marksman
content:
Karl David Mejillano boosted Brokenshire College Toril's (BCT) DAVRAA Meet 2026 medal collection with a remarkable SILVER medal performance in the Secondary Archery Team Shooting event.
Mejillano trained under in-house coach Ethan Alelojo.
Congratulations and we are proud of you, Brokenshirian!

title: Making Waves in the Regionals
content:
Huan Marth Dwyne Gerodiaz powered through the lanes to clinch a SILVER medal in the Secondary Swimming 4x50-meter Freestyle Relay to add to Brokenshire College Toril's (BCT) growing medal haul at the DAVRAA Meet 2026. 
Huan trained under in-house coach Elaiza Edar and Eduard Hamot Gultiano Jr. 
Congratulations and we are proud of you, Brokenshirian!

title: BCT Sprinters bolt to Palarong Pambansa
content:
Hauling an impressive collection of 3 silver medals and 1 bronze, Toril sports powerhouse Brokenshire College Toril (BCT) charges to the Palarong Pambansa after a commanding performance in the DAVRAA Meet 2026 Athletics Track and Field events.
Leading the surge is Hiro Osunwoke, who delivered a standout campaign on the track with a silver medal finish in the 100-meter dash, another silver as part of the 4x100-meter relay team, and a bronze medal in the 200-meter dash.
Joining him is Khrizia Shailey Nunal, who clinched silver in the 100-meter dash.
Now Grade 12 students, Nunal and Osunwoke secured their first-ever Palarong Pambansa berths in their final year of eligibility after years of competition, marking a breakthrough in their athletic careers.Nakahilak jud ko sa kalipay (I cried tears of joy), said Richpearl Nunal, BCT in-house coach and Khrizia's mother, as she reflected on the obstacles they overcame to reach the nationals.
The pair also trained under coaches Biverlie Tariao and Arinel Abarra.
Palarong Pambansa will take place on May 24-31, 2026 in Agusan Del Sur.

title: CHAMPION in the Modern Dance
content:
Brokenshire College Toril (BCT) Aces - NumeroUno blazed through the stage and stormed their way to the CHAMPIONSHIP in the Modern Dance SHS Category of the 2026 DAPRISSA Musical Fest.
Members include: Ryeden Jesse Bilbao, Yoanna Mai G. Uyamot, Dranreb James C. Javier, Steve Nash A. Clerigo, Dhaniel Jan D. Lumanta, Kristina Cassandra Cabatingan, Pepsi Jane J. Acesor, Honey Largo, Sophia Atasha Maunes, Renee Erika Gabrielle V. Delos Reyes, Krizza Mae G. Absin, and Hardy Gatela.
The team trained under in-house coaches Phoebe Kenrick Catinoy and Krystal Joy Bolasco.
Congratulations and we are proud of you, Brokenshirians!

title: Congratulations! BCT Robo Aces
content:
With a remarkable haul of eleven major awards, including the coveted overall championship, Brokenshire College Toril's (BCT) team of student roboticis also known as the Robo Aces rose above fierce competition to secure their place among the best robotics teams in the country during the 11th Philippine Robothon, held in Iloilo City last Saturday, February 7. Competing against top-performing schools from various regions nationwide, the Robo Aces, consisting of elementary, junior high school, and senior high school students, showcased exceptional skill, innovation, and teamwork across multiple robotics categories.
The teams victory reflects months of rigorous preparation, problem-solving under pressure, and a shared commitment to excellence.This year's haul further broadens BCT's growing collection of awards earned from local and international competitions since the school began participating in robotics events in 2018.
Robotics education has long been a flagship feature of the school's curriculum, equipping students with future-ready skills in programming, engineering design, critical thinking, and collaborative problem-solving. Through a strong emphasis on hands-on learning, innovation-driven instruction, and exposure to competitive platforms, the program empowers learners to apply classroom concepts to real-world challenges, reinforcing the school's commitment to delivering quality, relevant, and globally competitive education.
